title: CABCDE danger check for non-medics
category: Medical and first · Aid · Disaster · Health
summary: Medical-heavy survival entry for a simple order for noticing life threats without pretending to diagnose. It is designed for offline emergencies and does not replace professional care.
content: Use this entry to organize a simple order for noticing life threats without pretending to diagnose. Start with scene safety, hand hygiene, calm communication, and written timing. Keep the person comfortable, protect privacy, avoid risky experiments, and prepare a clear handover for a doctor, ambulance, clinic, pharmacist, or trained first-aider. Watch especially for: blocked airway, severe bleeding, weak breathing, confusion, pale cold skin, or worsening pain. In a long emergency, review the person regularly instead of assuming they are stable.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “CABCDE danger check for non-medics”.
soap or sanitizer if available — for «CABCDE danger check for non-medics»
gloves or clean barrier if available — for «CABCDE danger check for non-medics»
paper and pen. Context: “CABCDE danger check for non-medics”.
phone or radio if available — for «CABCDE danger check for non-medics»
labels for times and medicines — for «CABCDE danger check for non-medics»
steps: Check that the place is safe for the helper and the injured or sick person. Apply it specifically within the “CABCDE danger check for non-medics” procedure.
Call emergency help or send a reliable person for help when any red flag is present. Apply it specifically within the “CABCDE danger check for non-medics” procedure.
Write the time the problem started, what changed, medicines taken, allergies, and known conditions. Apply it specifically within the “CABCDE danger check for non-medics” procedure.
Use clean hands and clean barriers before touching wounds, body fluids, food, medicines, infants, or medical devices. Apply it specifically within the “CABCDE danger check for non-medics” procedure.
Keep the person warm or cool as appropriate, still if trauma is possible, and protected from crowds. Apply it specifically within the “CABCDE danger check for non-medics” procedure.
Recheck and record changes regularly so deterioration is noticed early. Apply it specifically within the “CABCDE danger check for non-medics” procedure.
Prepare a short handover note with name, age, symptoms, times, medicines, allergies, and actions already taken. Apply it specifically within the “CABCDE danger check for non-medics” procedure.
warnings: Do not use this as a replacement for training, emergency services, a clinician, or local medical guidance. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “CABCDE danger check for non-medics”.

Do not guess medicine doses, mix medicines without label checking, share prescriptions, or perform invasive procedures. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “CABCDE danger check for non-medics”.
Escalate urgently if you see: blocked airway, severe bleeding, weak breathing, confusion, pale cold skin, or worsening pain.
Keep children, elders, pregnancy, disability, immune weakness, and chronic illness in the high-risk group. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “CABCDE danger check for non-medics”.
common_mistakes: Waiting too long after red flags appear while carrying out “CABCDE danger check for non-medics”
Not writing times and medicines while carrying out “CABCDE danger check for non-medics”
Touching wounds or fluids without hand hygiene while carrying out “CABCDE danger check for non-medics”
Letting crowds block air, privacy, or transport while carrying out “CABCDE danger check for non-medics”
Assuming a quiet person is stable without rechecking while carrying out “CABCDE danger check for non-medics”
alternatives: Use official first-aid training when available as a fallback for “CABCDE danger check for non-medics”
Use local emergency numbers and pharmacists for medicine questions as a fallback for “CABCDE danger check for non-medics”
Use paper handover notes when phones are dead as a fallback for “CABCDE danger check for non-medics”
Use a trusted translator if language blocks care as a fallback for “CABCDE danger check for non-medics”
failure_signs: Red flags appear: blocked airway, severe bleeding, weak breathing, confusion, pale cold skin, or worsening pain
The person becomes harder to wake, breathe, drink, speak, walk, or recognize during “CABCDE danger check for non-medics”
Pain, bleeding, swelling, fever, confusion, or weakness gets worse during “CABCDE danger check for non-medics”
when_not_to_use: Do not delay emergency care to complete the checklist when using the “CABCDE danger check for non-medics” record
Do not move a trauma patient unless staying is more dangerous when using the “CABCDE danger check for non-medics” record
Do not force care on a conscious person except immediate life safety situations under local rules when using the “CABCDE danger check for non-medics” record
short_term: Stabilize the scene, reduce immediate harm, record key facts, and connect the person to trained help. This note applies specifically to CABCDE danger check for non-medics.
long_term: Keep medical records updated, train the group in certified first aid, maintain supplies, and practice respectful handovers. This note applies specifically to CABCDE danger check for non-medics.
if_method_fails: Escalate to emergency services, nearest clinic, pharmacist, radio medical consult, or evacuation route; document the failed step and time. This note applies specifically to CABCDE danger check for non-medics.
environment_notes: In apartments, heat, smoke, poor ventilation, crowds, no elevator, and water outages can make medical problems worse. Plan quiet space, clean water, light, and transport. This note applies specifically to CABCDE danger check for non-medics.
